name: Trail between Vaso and Espaldera (PRC-BU 27) — circular from Anguix
name_original: Sendero entre Vaso y Espaldera (PRC-BU 27) — circular desde Anguix
region_intro: Hiking around Anguix, located in the Burgos province of Spain, offers routes through a landscape characterized by river valleys, agricultural fields, and gentle hills. The region is part of the Ribera del Duero, known for its vineyards and traditional villages. Trails often follow river courses and traverse open countryside, providing views of the local flora and rural architecture. Best hiking trails around Anguix The most popular hiking route is The Hermitages of Sotillo de la Ribera — circular along the outskirts of the village, a 8.5 miles (13.6 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the surroundings of Sotillo de la Ribera, passing by local hermitages. Another top favourite among local hikers is Trail between Vaso and Espaldera (PRC-BU 27) — circular from Anguix, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.4 km) path. This circular route starts directly from Anguix, offering a convenient exploration of the local terrain. Local hikers also love the Part of the Mulberry and Valleys Trail - circular from Villovela de Esgueva, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through varied valley landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Anguix is defined by riverine paths, agricultural plains, and gentle ascents through rural Spanish countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Anguix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Anguix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Anguix?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes available around Anguix, offering a variety of experiences through river valleys, agricultural fields, and gentle hills. These trails are well-suited for exploring the rural Spanish countryside.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Anguix?
A: Hikes near Anguix typically feature riverine paths, agricultural plains, and gentle ascents through the characteristic landscape of the Ribera del Duero. You'll encounter vineyards and traditional villages, with routes often following river courses and traversing open countryside.

Q: Are there any circular walks starting directly from Anguix?
A: Yes, there is a convenient circular route starting directly from Anguix. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1124627919/trail-between-vaso-and-espaldera-prc-bu-27-circular-from-anguix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail between Vaso and Espaldera (PRC-BU 27)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.9-mile (9.4 km) path that allows for easy exploration of the local terrain.

Q: What are some interesting places to see along the hiking trails around Anguix?
A: While hiking around Anguix, you can explore several charming settlements and historical sites. Notable places include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5506336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tórtoles de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;, the village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5569849" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5506261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roa de Duero&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5506332" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel in Villovela de Esgueva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Anguix?
A: The hiking routes in Anguix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the peaceful rural settings, the varied terrain of riverine paths and agricultural fields, and the opportunity to explore traditional villages.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037329014/ribera-del-duero-alto-de-socastillo-y-san-martin-de-rubiales-circular-from-roa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Duero - Alto de Socastillo y San Martín de Rubiales&lt;/a&gt; route, starting from Roa, is a difficult 14.4-mile (23.1 km) trail. It offers a more extensive exploration of the region's landscapes.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by local hermitages?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040929648/the-hermitages-of-sotillo-de-la-ribera-circular-along-the-outskirts-of-the-village" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitages of Sotillo de la Ribera&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 8.5-mile (13.6 km) circular trail that specifically explores the surroundings of Sotillo de la Ribera, passing by local hermitages.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Esgueva valley?
A: Yes, you can explore the Esgueva valley on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037354194/part-of-the-mulberry-and-valleys-trail-circular-from-villovela-de-esgueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Part of the Mulberry and Valleys Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a circular route from Villovela de Esgueva. This 8.1-mile (13.0 km) trail leads through varied valley landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking around Anguix?
A: Given the region's agricultural character and river valleys, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be warm, while winters are cooler, but trails remain accessible for those prepared for the weather.

Q: Are there any trails near reservoirs?
A: Yes, you can find a route that takes you around the Tórtoles de Esgueva Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282068811/return-to-the-tortoles-de-esgueva-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Tórtoles de Esgueva Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) hike offering views of the water body.

Q: What kind of flora and rural architecture can I expect to see?
A: The trails around Anguix provide views of local flora typical of agricultural and riverine environments, alongside traditional rural architecture found in the villages and scattered throughout the countryside. You'll experience the authentic charm of the Ribera del Duero region.
tips:
Small town in Burgos with less than 200 inhabitants in the region of La Ribera.
The parish church of the Asunción de Nuestra Señora, dating from the 18th century, and the Hermitage of San Juan, from 1737, stand out.
Beautiful Catholic church dating from the 18th century and baroque style inside. The beautiful main entrance with a semicircular arch and the tower with the bell tower stand out.

waypoints.name: Church of the Assumption of Our Lady
